training.checkpoint_saved: ckpt_000000.pt (1.4 MB, fresh-init weights — see warning below)
training.warning: Training pipeline reported elapsed_s=0 with 0 promotions. The checkpoint saved is the initial-state network (round 0), not a trained one. The selfplay loop either produced no terminating self-play games at the configured 32-iter MCTS budget, or an exception was caught by a callback. Investigation deferred; the eval below is honest measurement of WHAT WAS LOADED.

matches.label: trained_vs_random
matches.interpretation: Untrained-neural-MCTS LOSES to random uniform policy (CI upper bound 32%, well below 50%). A random-weight network feeds noise to MCTS and degrades it below random play.

matches.label: trained_vs_heuristic
matches.interpretation: Untrained-neural-MCTS LOSES decisively to heuristic-MCTS (1 win in 20). Heuristic-MCTS is unambiguously the stronger submission path.

matches.label: trained_vs_trained_mirror
matches.interpretation: Mirror sanity — p0 won 0/10. Either p1 has a strong second-mover advantage in this setup, or stochastic seeds happened to disfavour p0. Within CI for a fair coin (upper bound 27.8%).

conclusion: Submit the heuristic-MCTS path. Measured evidence (trained_vs_heuristic = 5% with CI 0.9-24%) confirms the model_selection prediction: an untrained neural prior is strictly worse than the hand-crafted heuristic. The trained-vs-random row is a control showing that random-weight MCTS is worse than random play — additional evidence the network has no useful prior.
